lead_profile_text: Existing property switching from personal/vacant use to STL. 1-bed flat, 7 Cranbrook Road, Flat 3, Bristol BS6 7BJ. Owned 11 years. Currently empty — Hannah living at boyfriend's in Wimbledon Hill. Fully furnished (Scandi style), personal items to remove. Managed block with 4 neighbours; resident management company in place. Lease residential use clause issue. Kitchen needs work (new floor, general tidying).
emotional_profile: Thoughtful and cautious — explicitly flagged 'early stage'. Primary objective is covering costs; confirmed figures achieve this. Not in a rush (no mortgage dependency on this income). Structural and legal concerns dominate. Engaged with Ltd company structure. Likes Slack. Patient lead — do not push urgency.
delay_trigger_phrases: Just to manage your expectations — it's really early stage
My neighbours and management company may object to short-term letting
delay_timeline: No confirmed start date; follow-up from 8th July 2026. Ltd company setup, lease/neighbour resolution, and kitchen work all outstanding.
green_light_trigger_phrases: Goal number one is covering costs — confirmed figures achieve this

top_objections.text: My neighbours and management company may object to short-term letting
top_objections.zac_response: Ltd company as trading entity holds tenancy agreement; applies for business rates via VOA. Personal ownership stays personal — no stamp duty issue on remortgage.

top_objections.text: There are residential use clauses in my lease — what do people typically do?
top_objections.zac_response: Ltd company structure: trading entity holds the STL agreement, not personal lease. VOA business rates registration removes council tax risk.

top_objections.text: I want weekday-only lets to avoid neighbour issues and attract professional/corporate guests
top_objections.zac_response: Flagged income limitation of weekday-only. Recommended Minut noise/occupancy sensor as alternative to weekday-only restriction.

top_objections.text: What about Bristol's proposed 90-day cap — is that going to be a problem?
top_objections.zac_response: Not yet enforceable. Only Greater London has a firm rule. Councils likely to move to licensing rather than outright bans.

top_objections.text: Are there upfront costs beyond the management fee?
top_objections.zac_response: Photography ~£250 paid separately. No onboarding or setup fee from Stayful.

top_questions.text: Can I do weekday-only letting to avoid neighbour issues?
top_questions.answer: Possible but limits income significantly. Minut noise/occupancy sensor recommended as alternative — monitors guest behaviour without restricting booking days.
Not yet enforceable. Only Greater London has a firm rule. Councils likely to move to licensing rather than outright bans on STL.

top_questions.text: How do I handle the residential use clause in my lease?
top_questions.answer: Ltd company as trading entity holds the tenancy agreement; applies for business rates via VOA. Personal ownership stays personal — no stamp duty issue on remortgage.

top_questions.text: What standard does the kitchen need to be?
top_questions.answer: Functionality prioritised over aesthetics. Professional photography is the key listing differentiator.
Photography ~£250 paid separately by Hannah. No onboarding or setup fee from Stayful.

what_moved_them: Income figures cover costs — confirmed as satisfying her primary objective
Minut noise/occupancy sensor approach — 'that's interesting, I don't know' (positive signal, engaged with the idea)
Ltd company / tenancy agreement structure — 'I'll explore that' (actively useful, new to her)
Slack communication — 'I love Slack, so I was very pleased to see it'
Professional photography as the key listing differentiator
what_fell_flat: Weekday-only letting not fully accommodated — Hannah's initial preference can't be optimally met without significant income sacrifice

conversion_gaps.gap: Lease clause and neighbour/management company approval needed before onboarding
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Provide Ltd company setup guidance in follow-up email; confirm VOA business rates process

conversion_gaps.gap: Ltd company not yet set up for this purpose
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Recommend she speaks to accountant; offer to refer if needed

conversion_gaps.gap: Kitchen work outstanding before photography can proceed
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Confirm minimum standard for onboarding — functionality over aesthetics; photography can proceed with minor kitchen work in progress

offer_framing: No offer made. Patient lead — do not push urgency. Mirror her language: 'covering costs', 'privacy', 'professional tenants'.
zac_income_intro: Annual gross £51,120 / annual net £26,585 (~£2,215/month). Year 1 conservative 80% ramp: £21,268 net. LTL comparison £14,785/year. STL uplift +£11,800/year (+80%). Worst month Jan £1,657 net. Best month Jul £2,747 net.
zac_reassurance_phrases: Bristol 90-day cap not yet enforceable — only Greater London has a firm rule
Councils likely to move to licensing rather than outright bans
VOA business rates registration avoids double council tax risk
Ramp-up caveat: 18 months to 2 years to reach consistent top-of-market rates — acknowledged from the outset
zac_analogies: Minut noise/occupancy sensor as an alternative to weekday-only restrictions — monitors behaviour without limiting bookings
Ltd company as trading entity holds tenancy agreement — personal ownership stays personal, no stamp duty issue on remortgage
